title: Dungeon Crusade - Part One - Welcome to Avalon!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4VN65sXHXSU

Daniel (@dungeondive) opens his series on **Dungeon Crusade Book One: Genesis of Evil** with full disclosure — he and designer Roger Pearce are online friends and have plans to collaborate, so he commits up front to praise where deserved and criticism where needed.

Rather than retread Roger's exhaustive coverage on his own channel, Daniel frames his series around *how he is actually approaching the game on a small table*: a custom character binder (one page per hero with abilities and gear in pockets), skipping the village board entirely, and corralling everything in a card tray. The bulk of the video then turns to the **Avalon Adventure Board Game** — the choose-your-own-adventure "appetizer" you play before the main course. You roam Avalon, hunt the three Stones of Eternity hidden among false stones, roll a d12 (or d6 in already-explored areas) at each location, and trigger encounters from a stack of cards.

He flags the wordy rulebook, slightly thin cards, and the just-a-hair-too-long-for-penny-sleeves dimension, but the standees with their thick black outlines stop him in his tracks — the best he's ever seen in a game. He closes by calling Dungeon Crusade exactly the kind of personal, decade-long passion project Kickstarter was built for.

----

Roger's avalon adventure board game is sold as an appetizer that teaches the world before the main game starts. Do you think a game-before-the-game adds atmosphere, or does it just delay the thing you actually came to play?
